query: How do users develop mental models of AI systems?

1. Search for academic papers on mental models and AI systems in HCI
2. Find web resources on UX design and mental models
3. Look for studies on user cognition when interacting with AI
4. Gather information on how interface design supports mental model formation
5. Collect evidence on trust, feedback, and transparency in AI interfaces

Key search queries:
- 'mental models AI systems HCI user study'
- 'user cognition AI interface design'
- 'AI transparency mental model formation'
- 'UX design AI mental models trust'

User prior technology experience significantly shapes AI mental model development.

# Final Response: How Users Develop Mental Models of AI Systems

Mental models are internal representations or understandings that users develop about how systems, particularly artificial intelligence (AI) systems, work. Given the increasing integration of AI into daily life, understanding how users develop these mental models is essential for creating effective, user-friendly, and transparent AI technologies.

## User Experience and AI

User experience (UX) plays a significant role in shaping mental models of AI systems. The design and functionality of an AI system can influence user perceptions and understanding. A well-designed interface that provides clear feedback can help users develop accurate mental models of how the system operates. According to the Interaction Design Foundation (IxDF), understanding mental models is crucial in UX/UI design and AI [1].

## Developing Mental Models through Interaction and Trust

Users develop mental models of AI systems through their interactions with these systems. Trust is a critical factor in this process — users are more likely to develop positive and accurate mental models of systems they trust. Combining AI with mental model frameworks can enhance UX design decisions [2].

## Factors Influencing Mental Models

Several factors influence mental model development: complexity of the AI system, user expertise level, and interface design. AI tools for UX designers are helping bridge the gap between rising expectations and limited time [3].

## Case Studies: Real-World Examples

Understanding what types of technology users have previously experienced can accelerate or hinder changes in their mental models of AI, as noted by EPIC People [4].

## Conclusion

Understanding how users develop mental models of AI systems is crucial for creating effective, user-friendly, and transparent AI technologies.

## References

[1] IxDF. (2026). What are Mental Models? Retrieved from https://ixdf.org/literature/topics/mental-models
[2] Nathan. (n.d.). Stop Overthinking UX Decisions. Retrieved from https://medium.com/@Nathan78/stop-overthinking-ux-decisions-how-ai-7-mental-models-can-10x-your-design-speed-558e2f6673db
[3] Figma. (2026). Top AI Tools for UX Designers. Retrieved from https://www.figma.com/resource-library/ai-tools-for-ux-designers/
[4] EPIC People. (n.d.). AI Mental Models & Trust. Retrieved from https://www.epicpeople.org/ai-mental-models-and-trust/
